widgets_values: I tried being productive today. I made a to-do list, got a coffee, opened my laptop… and then spent 45 minutes researching whether penguins have knees. They do, by the way. So technically, I learned something. That counts as education, not procrastination. Then I cleaned my room by moving everything from the chair to the bed, which means I now have nowhere to sit or sleep. Honestly, I’m thriving. Tomorrow I’ll fix it. Or I’ll research whether bears can use Wi-Fi. Priorities.

I tried being productive today. I made a to-do list, got a coffee, opened my laptop… and then spent 45 minutes researching whether penguins have knees. They do, by the way. So technically, I learned something. That counts as education, not procrastination. Then I cleaned my room by moving everything from the chair to the bed, which means I now have nowhere to sit or sleep. Honestly, I’m thriving. Tomorrow I’ll fix it. Or I’ll research whether bears can use Wi-Fi. Priorities.
This is the sentence ending or continuation to generate extra audio based on the reference speaker's voice
MOSS-TTS Local Transformer v1.5 BF16 - OpenMOSS-Team (auto-download)

## Moss_TTS-ComfyUI Model Requirements

**VRAM:** MOSS-TTS Local Transformer v1.5 BF16 stages roughly **8.7 GB** for the main model and **4.0 GB** for MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2. With AIMDO DynamicVRAM, pages move into VRAM as the model runs, so active VRAM can grow during generation / codec encode-decode. Leave extra VRAM for KV cache, Whisper ASR, ComfyUI, and other loaded models.

Moss_TTS-ComfyUI has **ComfyUI / AIMDO model-management integration** with unload support, real tensor registration, and cleanup/reload when model, dtype, or attention settings change.

**MOSS-TTS main TTS model**

- 🤗 [OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 — model.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5/resolve/main/model.safetensors?download=true)

**Required audio tokenizer**

- 🤗 [OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2)

**Optional Whisper ASR for continuation transcripts**

- 🤗 [openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo](https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo)
- 🤗 [openai/whisper-large-v3](https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-large-v3)
- 🤗 [openai/whisper-medium](https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-medium)
- 🤗 [openai/whisper-small](https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-small)
- 🤗 [openai/whisper-tiny](https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-tiny)

**Required model assets**

- `moss-tts-local-transformer-v1.5/model.safetensors`
- `moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/model.safetensors.index.json`
- `moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/model-00001-of-00003.safetensors`
- `moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/model-00002-of-00003.safetensors`
- `moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/model-00003-of-00003.safetensors`

Assets are downloaded from:

- 🤗 [OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5)

and

- 🤗 [OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2)

## Model Storage Location

~~~text
ComfyUI/models/mosstts/
├── moss-tts-local-transformer-v1.5/
│   └── model.safetensors
└── moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/
    ├── model.safetensors.index.json
    ├── model-00001-of-00003.safetensors
    ├── model-00002-of-00003.safetensors
    └── model-00003-of-00003.safetensors
~~~

Optional Whisper ASR models are stored under:

~~~text
ComfyUI/models/audio_encoders/
└── openai_whisper-large-v3-turbo/
    ├── config.json
    ├── generation_config.json
    ├── model.safetensors
    ├── preprocessor_config.json
    ├── tokenizer.json
    └── tokenizer_config.json
~~~

If `download_if_missing` is enabled, missing model files / tokenizer files are downloaded automatically.

The loader uses bundled architecture, config, processor, and tokenizer assets from:

~~~text
ComfyUI/custom_nodes/Moss_TTS-ComfyUI/assets/
├── moss-tts-local-transformer-v1.5/
└── moss-audio-tokenizer-v2/
~~~

## Notes

- Use `dtype: auto`, `bf16`, or `fp16`.
- Use `attention: auto`, `sdpa`, `flash_attention`, or `eager`.
- `auto` selects FlashAttention when available, otherwise SDPA on CUDA, and eager on CPU.
- MOSS-TTS v1.5 outputs **48 kHz stereo audio**.
- Voice cloning uses audio-only reference input and does **not** require a transcript.
- Speech continuation requires `prefix_audio` plus a matching `prefix_text`.
- The optional Whisper node can generate `prefix_text` for continuation workflows.
- Recommended voice reference: **5–30 seconds** of clean, single-speaker speech.
- MOSS supports inline pause tags such as `[pause 3.2s]`.
- Supported controls include language tags, duration tokens, Pinyin / IPA pronunciation markup, multilingual synthesis, and code-switching.
- With AIMDO enabled, RAM usage is expected because AIMDO keeps CPU/host-backed weight storage while faulting active pages into VRAM.

## Links

- [Issues](https://github.com/Saganaki22/Moss_TTS-ComfyUI/issues)
- [Moss_TTS-ComfyUI](https://github.com/Saganaki22/Moss_TTS-ComfyUI)
- [MOSS-TTS Local Transformer v1.5 Model Repository](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5)
- [MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 Repository](https://huggingface.co/OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2)
- [Official OpenMOSS/MOSS-TTS Repository](https://github.com/OpenMOSS/MOSS-TTS)
- [MOSS-TTS Technical Report](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18090)

## MOSS-TTS Field Guide

MOSS-TTS uses separate structured fields. Users should not put everything into `instruction`.

| Field | Use For | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | Actual words to speak | Dialogue, narration, `[pause 1.2s]`, Pinyin / IPA |
| `instruction` | Performance / delivery | emotion, pace, accent, narrator style |
| `quality` | Recording quality | clean studio speech, broadcast quality |
| `sound_event` | Foreground vocal/audio event | soft laugh, sigh, gasp, breath |
| `ambient_sound` | Background environment | quiet room tone, soft rain, distant city ambience |

## `quality`

Use for fidelity / cleanliness / recording style.

- clean studio speech
- high fidelity voice recording
- broadcast quality narration
- natural clean speech
- clear voice recording with minimal noise
- professional audiobook recording
- close-mic studio vocal

## `sound_event`

Use for foreground events, especially vocal events.

- soft laugh
- gentle sigh
- brief inhale before speaking
- quiet chuckle
- small gasp
- clears throat softly
- whispered breath

## `ambient_sound`

Use for background environment.

- quiet room tone
- subtle studio ambience
- soft rain outside
- distant city ambience
- quiet office background
- gentle cafe ambience
- faint outdoor wind

## `instruction`

Use for how the speaker performs the line.

- Speak in a calm, natural audiobook narrator style.
- Use a warm conversational tone with medium pacing.
- Sound excited but still realistic and clear.
- Read slowly with careful pronunciation.
- Use a serious documentary narrator delivery.
- Continue seamlessly from the reference audio with the same speaker style.

## Complete Examples

| Use | `text` | `instruction` | `quality` | `sound_event` | `ambient_sound` |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Clean Narration | The door opened slowly, and the room beyond it was filled with pale blue light. | Read as a calm audiobook narrator with gentle suspense. | clean studio speech |  |  |
| Voice Clone, Natural | Thanks for listening. I will walk you through the setup step by step. | Match the reference speaker naturally with relaxed pacing. | natural clean speech |  | quiet room tone |
| Emotional Line | I never thought we would make it this far, but here we are. | Speak with gentle relief and quiet emotion. | clean voice recording | soft sigh |  |
| Atmospheric Scene | I found the letter on the kitchen table just after midnight. | Speak softly, like telling someone a secret. | natural close-mic speech | brief inhale before speaking | soft rain outside |

## Rule Of Thumb

- `text`: spoken words
- `instruction`: acting / delivery
- `quality`: recording cleanliness
- `sound_event`: foreground event
- `ambient_sound`: background environment
- Leave unused fields blank.
